channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Amazon deepens AI push with $25 billion bet on Anthropic
item.description: Amazon's $25 billion investment in Anthropic boosts AI capabilities, securing cloud commitments and expanding infrastructure for enhanced enterprise solutions. [Amazon, Anthropic]

item.title: Anthropic and Status of AI Data Centers
item.description: Amazon announced an additional $5 billion investment in Anthropic immediately, plus up to $20 billion more in the future (tied to commercial milestones). This brings its total committed capital to as much as $33 billion. The announcement does not specify how … [Amazon, Anthropic]

item.title: Meta breaks ground on over $1 billion data center in Oklahoma's Tulsa
item.description: April 21 : Meta Platforms said on Tuesday it has begun construction on a new data center in Tulsa, Oklahoma, a project valued at more than $1 billion, as the Facebook-parent races to build AI infrastructure for securing computing power.Like its Big Tech peers… [Meta]

item.title: Core Scientific plans $3.3B debt raise to fund AI data center push
item.description: Core Scientific is raising $3.3 billion through secured notes to refinance debt and expand data center operations, reflecting a broader shift among crypto miners toward AI infrastructure.

item.title: 5 Reasons Navitas Semiconductor Stock Is Exploding Higher in 2026
item.description: TORRANCE, Calif. — Navitas Semiconductor Corp. shares exploded more than 18% in early trading Tuesday, reaching $15.66 as momentum built around the company's gallium nitride and silicon carbide power solutions for artificial intelligence data centers and ener… [Intel]

item.title: TSMC can't keep up with AI chip demand, with shortages projected to last beyond 2027
item.description: The AI boom has driven massive silicon orders, and despite ramping up production, TSMC warns that chip shortages will persist through 2027 and beyond.Continue reading at TweakTown [TSMC]
